file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: This video will help you understand a heart attack and how to manage it.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Please watch the entire video to learn how to manage your recovery.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: If you have been hospitalized with a heart attack, it's important to recover properly when

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: you return home.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: A heart attack, also called a myocardial infarction, or MI, occurs when blood flow to an area of

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: your heart is greatly reduced.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: When the blood supply is not able to keep up with the demand, the heart muscle cells don't

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: get enough blood to stay alive.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: The result is death of heart muscle cells in the affected area of the heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: The goals after a heart attack are to keep your heart healthy and avoid another heart attack.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Recovery takes time and the amount is different for each patient.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: It depends upon how much of your heart muscle is damaged and your individual rate of recovery.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: You may also have had a procedure.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: This may also affect your recovery time.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: You can lower your risk of having another heart attack by taking these steps, follow your

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: doctor's instructions, make some lifestyle changes, and know when to seek medical attention.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Following the instructions of your doctor and healthcare team is very important to your recovery.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: This includes taking your medications exactly as prescribed by your doctor.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Don't stop taking your medications or skip doses.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Take note of any side effects and tell your doctor.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Don't take any over-the-counter medication or supplement without first consulting your doctor.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Tell your doctor about any other prescriptions you're taking.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Some medications can prevent your heart medicine from working.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: It's important that your recovery is monitored.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Keep all of your follow-up appointments.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Have exams done as directed by your doctor.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: For example, if your doctor has told you to check your blood pressure regularly, do

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: as directed.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Lowering your risk of another heart attack usually requires some lifestyle changes.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Limit your salt and sodium intake by doing the following.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Don't add salt when you cook or to food at the table.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Avoid processed and fast foods.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Compare food labels and choose the items that are lower in salt and sodium.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Avoid a heart-healthy diet, including low fat, low cholesterol, and high fiber foods.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: A heart-healthy diet helps you maintain or achieve a good blood pressure and weight.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: It also helps prevent other conditions that may further stress your heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Limit your alcohol intake.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Risk your doctor if it's safe to include any alcohol in your diet.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Maintain a healthy weight.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Don't smoke.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Avoid all tobacco products and secondhand smoke.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Nicotine increases heart rate and blood pressure.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: At the same time, it decreases the amount of oxygen-rich blood delivered to your body's

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: tissues.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Allow yourself time to heal by taking it easy for the first four weeks.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Getting a good night's sleep.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Rest in the afternoon.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Avoid heavy lifting and gradually increasing your physical activity.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Ask your doctor when you can resume driving, sexual activity, and return to work.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: For weeks after your heart attack, you will begin cardiac rehab, which includes a closely

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: monitored exercise regimen.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: After completing the cardiac rehab program, continue with regular exercise through daily

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: activities such as light yard and housework.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Other good exercise options with your doctor's approval are walking, swimming, and bicycling.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: As you exercise, keep these precautions in mind.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Rest as needed.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Stop if you feel chest pain or have shortness of breath.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: And don't exercise when temperatures are very hot or very cold.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Stress can affect your blood pressure and your ability to maintain a healthy lifestyle.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Identifying the causes of your stress and learning to eliminate or manage them can help

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: with your recovery.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: You may feel depressed.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Just these feelings with your doctor.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: He or she can prescribe medicine, counseling, or a support group to help you adjust.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: It is important to stay aware of changes in your symptoms and know when to get help.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: If your hospital stay included a procedure with an incision, check the site each day for

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: any signs of infection.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Call your doctor if you develop a fever or redness, heat, pain, tenderness, pus, or bleeding

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: at the incision site.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Be mindful of any symptoms of chest pain, pressure, tightness, heaviness, ache, or any other

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: kind of unusual sensation in your chest as any of these could mean you have a blockage

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: in the blood vessels of the heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Symptoms related to blockage in the blood vessels of the heart are called angina and could

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: be any one of these symptoms.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Angina symptoms may also be felt in your arms, shoulders, neck, jaw, throat, or back.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Call your doctor if you think you're experiencing angina.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Call 911 immediately if you have any of these symptoms.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Angina that doesn't go away with medication is prescribed by your doctor.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Shortness of breath.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Cold sweats.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: nausea.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Vomiting.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
text_en: Or lightheadedness.
